spec_type_rationale: Zero-behaviour-change visual-fidelity pass: behaviour preserved (onSend/⌘↵/⌘S/canSend/dirty/per-tab/data-mstyle untouched), CSS+presentational markup restructured. Save/Share labels + <kbd> ⌘↵ are presentational edits to the existing RequestBar organism, not new type-defs/api-ops/components. Mirrors 005 fidelity refactor.

findings.content: Only Shell.tsx writes documentElement data-mstyle/data-theme/data-accent; RequestBar must NEVER write data-mstyle — the method-pill colour fix stays class-based.
Never use inline styles — class-based styling composed with cx(); all fidelity changes land in RequestBar.css + className edits.
Prefer design tokens (CSS custom properties in tokens.css) over literal style values; bind radius/colour/shadow to existing tokens.
Search before building — reuse the canonical .method/{METHOD} convention (Tabs.tsx:117 methodChipClassName) rather than inventing a new method-colour mechanism.

Playwright CT fidelity tests must reproduce the full styling context: import tokens.css, set data-mstyle on the host, and apply the production className scope (e.g. .request-bar) — else computed-style assertions read wrong values.
Radix overlay (method Dropdown) CT dismiss tests must await overlay readiness + yield setTimeout(0) before page.mouse.click — the DismissableLayer pointerdown listener arms via setTimeout(0).
Fidelity work is CT-verified in a real browser (jsdom can't resolve pseudo-element/computed styles); a stale CT build cache can mask a clean source as broken — clear caches before editing source.
Renderer is React 19 atomic-design; RequestBar is a flat organism under organisms/; styling = semantic classes bound to tokens.css, per-component sibling CSS file, no inline styles.
Type-check: npm run typecheck:web; lint: eslint --cache .; build: npm run build; unit: vitest; CT: playwright. Tests must stay green.
design_token_provenance forcing-function: a static design-token provenance check runs when a design manifest exists; UI values should trace to tokens.
Shell Effect 1 is sole writer of data-mstyle (default 'soft'); per-method .method colour resolves at runtime via [data-mstyle] ancestor — RequestBar inherits it but cannot write it.
Method chip uses unprefixed global .method/.{METHOD} classes so tokens.css colour rules apply project-wide; adding a .method rule affects ALL chip instances (TabBar too).

findings.source_section: Hazard: HEAD under chip mstyle / tokens.css:109
findings.content: tokens.css has un-gated .method.HEAD{color} but the GET..OPTIONS un-gated defaults are absent; HEAD rendering degrades under some mstyle variants — known fragility.
Renderer test stack: Vitest+TL (jsdom) for interaction; Playwright CT (Chromium) for real-browser focus/keyboard + computed-style fidelity; Radix overlay CT needs the two-step dismiss gate.

Reference geometry (design/styles.css): .reqbar gap 8px/padding 12px 16px; .url-bar + .btn height 32px, radius --radius(7px), focus ring box-shadow 0 0 0 3px accent14%; .btn-primary weight 600 + inset shadow; .method-select bordered 88px box w/ bold 11.5px plain-coloured method text; .kbd ⌘↵ keycap; .btn-ghost bordered actions.

Component drift (RequestBar.css): uniform height 24px, --radius-sm(5px), focus = border-color only (no ring), Send weight 500/no shadow, soft-chip method treatment, Save/Share icon-only. All needed tokens already exist in tokens.css.
Root cause is token-APPLICATION local to RequestBar.css, not a token gap or logic defect — localised CSS rewrite + small presentational markup (⌘↵ kbd, action labels).

Runner-up token-gap (un-gated .method.GET..OPTIONS defaults dropped in migration) is falsified for in-app (data-mstyle='soft' colours the pill) — only bites no-data-mstyle isolation; deferred as optional design-system follow-up.
Confirm exact reference geometry/treatment via runtime design-auditor screenshot + computed-style diff before locking RequestBar.css values (code-confirmed, not yet runtime-verified).
009 shipped functional RequestBar: method Dropdown + URL input + Send/Save/Share, ⌘↵/⌘S, canSend trim guard, updateActiveSpec, per-tab isolation — all behaviour to PRESERVE unchanged.
009 AC-6: renderer keeps Shell as sole writer of data-mstyle — binding invariant carried into 010.

009 §7 already mandated: design/reference.html is look-only (never reproduce data-om-*/__OmT/tweaks-panel markup); verify fidelity via computed-style equality + thresholded screenshot diff per 005; CT fixtures import tokens.css + set data-mstyle + Radix two-step gate.

009 RequestBar class structure: .request-bar, .request-bar__method (+ .method/{METHOD}), .request-bar__url, .request-bar__send, .request-bar__save(--dirty), .request-bar__share — the semantic class surface 010 restyles.

005 is the fidelity-verification template: computed-style EXACT equality on enumerated props in Playwright CT (resolves ::before/::after) + thresholded screenshot diff with explicit anti-aliasing pixel-threshold; runtime design-auditor (Chrome MCP) supplementary.
005 scoped new geometry CSS under .tabbar so the other Tabs consumer wasn't regressed; 010 analogue: scope restyle under .request-bar so no other component regresses.
005 deferred the exact screenshot-diff anti-aliasing pixel-threshold to /plan and mandated an explicit threshold must be set (no eyeballing); same pattern applies to 010.

decision_points.description: Fidelity fix scope: localised RequestBar.css(+markup) only, vs also restore un-gated tokens.css .method defaults (design-system ripple)
decision_points.user_answer: Localised RequestBar.css(+markup) now (Approach 1); restore of un-gated tokens.css .method defaults is deferred to a separate follow-up (open question/bug), NOT done in 010. No tokens.css/tokens.json change in this feature.

decision_points.description: Save/Share actions: add visible text labels matching reference, vs keep icon-only with aria-label
decision_points.user_answer: Visible 'Save'/'Share' text labels beside icons, laid-out .btn-ghost actions per reference (32px/bordered geometry still applies). Drop now-redundant aria-label where a visible text label gives the accessible name (no double-labelling). Share stays the 009 AC-19 disabled/no-op stub — labelled + in final slot, still disabled. Actions don't reflow on long URL (009 AC-20 preserved).

decision_points.description: Send ⌘↵ affordance: render a visible kbd keycap inside the active Send button, vs keep the shortcut with no visible keycap
decision_points.user_answer: Presentational <kbd> ⌘↵ inside Send per reference .kbd keycap; aria-hidden (decorative — button accessible name stays 'Send'); rendered ONLY in the active/enabled (canSend) state, not the disabled empty-URL state; styled from tokens. Display-only — does NOT register/alter any shortcut; the 009 ⌘Enter canSend-guarded onSend path is unchanged.

decision_points.description: no relevant decision point for breaking_changes
no relevant decision point for existing_behavior
no relevant decision point for data_flow_state
no relevant decision point for edge_cases
no relevant decision point for tooling_configuration

overview: Close the visual-fidelity gap between the RequestBar organism (feature 009) and design/reference.html's request bar, in BOTH the empty and filled states, with ZERO behaviour change. 009 shipped functionally correct but visually drifted: the bar is too cramped (uniform 24px geometry vs the reference's 32px), the URL input's radius + focus treatment are wrong, the method pill reads as a faint soft chip rather than the reference's bordered method-select with bold coloured text, the active Send lacks the solid-primary weight + ⌘↵ keycap, and Save/Share are bare undersized icons rather than laid-out labelled actions. 010 is a localised RequestBar.css restyle plus small presentational markup edits (visible Save/Share labels, an aria-hidden ⌘↵ keycap), binding to existing tokens.css custom-properties — no token additions, no logic change.
current_state: RequestBar (src/renderer/src/components/organisms/RequestBar.tsx:105-327 + sibling RequestBar.css) renders the [method ▾][URL][Send/Save/Share] bar wired to tabsStore via per-field selectors (009, Complete). RequestBar.css applies a uniform 24px geometry: .request-bar padding 5px 8px / gap 6px (RequestBar.css:48-57); all controls height 24px with border-radius var(--radius-sm) (5px); .request-bar__url focus sets border-color only, no ring (RequestBar.css:158-161); .request-bar__send is accent-fill weight 500, no shadow and no ⌘↵ element (RequestBar.css:222-246); Save/Share are icon-only buttons (RequestBar.tsx:283-323). The method trigger uses cx('request-bar__method','method',method) (RequestBar.tsx:256) so per-method colour resolves through tokens.css's [data-mstyle='soft'] .method.GET rules — Shell is the sole writer of data-mstyle (default 'soft', settingsStore.ts:65; Shell.tsx Effect 1), so the in-app pill IS coloured (009 AC-6). The reference target values live in design/styles.css: .reqbar gap 8px / padding 12px 16px (770-776), .url-bar height 32px / radius var(--radius) / focus ring box-shadow 0 0 0 3px accent14% (801-816), .btn-primary weight 600 + inset shadow (851-860), .method-select bordered 88px box with bold 11.5px plain-coloured method text (785-799) over un-gated .method.GET{color} (490-516), .kbd ⌘↵ keycap (245-254), .btn-ghost bordered actions (838-849). All radius/colour/shadow/font tokens the reference needs already exist in tokens.css. App.tsx:23 mounts <RequestBar /> (default no-op onSend). Research: research/2026-06-28-requestbar-009-visual-drift.md confirms the drift is token-APPLICATION local to RequestBar.css (not a token gap). The existing CT suite (RequestBar.ct.tsx) covers layout/behaviour but has NO computed-style fidelity assertions; its fixtures already import tokens.css + set data-mstyle='soft' + apply the Radix two-step dismiss gate (the 005 CT-fixture-scoping pattern).
desired_behavior: RequestBar shall match design/reference.html's request bar in BOTH the empty and filled states, verified by tier (computed-style EXACT equality on enumerated props in Playwright CT + a thresholded screenshot diff, per the 005 precedent), with ZERO behaviour change. (1) Bar geometry: .request-bar adopts the reference padding/gap and a ~32px control height; controls bind border-radius to var(--radius) (7px) where the reference uses it. (2) URL input: matching radius and a focus treatment that adds the accent ring (box-shadow 0 0 0 3px of an accent-14% mix) alongside the accent border. (3) Method pill: presents the reference's bordered method-select treatment (border, elevated background, --radius, bold ~11.5px coloured method text) via a LOCAL .request-bar__method override of the inherited soft chip — keeping the per-method colour on the existing .method/.{METHOD} class path and NEVER writing data-mstyle (009 AC-6). (4) Send active state: solid-primary fill at weight 600 plus the reference inset-highlight shadow, plus a presentational aria-hidden <kbd> ⌘↵ keycap rendered ONLY in the enabled (canSend) state — display-only, registering no shortcut. (5) Save/Share: laid-out .btn-ghost-style buttons (bordered, ~32px) with VISIBLE 'Save'/'Share' text labels beside their icons; where the visible text supplies the accessible name, the now-redundant aria-label is dropped (no double-labelling); Share stays the 009 AC-19 disabled/no-op stub, labelled and in its final slot. (6) The empty-state Send greyed-disabled appearance is correct canSend-guard behaviour and is preserved, not restyled away. Fidelity is rebuilt with semantic classes bound to tokens.css (values from design/tokens.json), NEVER by importing design-export markup/cruft (data-om-*, __OmT, tweaks-panel). The new restyle is scoped under .request-bar so no other component regresses. All 009 behaviour is untouched (RequestSpec read/write, onSend, ⌘Enter/⌘S, dirty/markClean, canSend trim guard, per-tab isolation, Shell-sole-writer-of-data-mstyle); existing unit + CT tests stay green and new computed-style fidelity CT is added; typecheck + lint + build pass.

affected_areas.impact: Modify — restyle to reference fidelity using existing tokens: reference padding/gap + ~32px control height; bind --radius (7px) where reference uses it; add URL-input accent focus-ring box-shadow; Send solid-primary weight 600 + inset shadow + .kbd keycap styling; local .request-bar__method override presenting the bordered method-select treatment (no data-mstyle); .btn-ghost-style labelled Save/Share. All rules scoped under .request-bar.

Modify — presentational only: add visible Save/Share text labels beside icons (drop now-redundant aria-label where visible text supplies the accessible name); add an aria-hidden kbd keycap inside Send rendered only in the enabled state; className/structure edits to match the reference. NO logic change — onSend, Cmd-Enter/Cmd-S, canSend, dirty/markClean, updateActiveSpec, per-tab selectors, data-mstyle invariant untouched.

Create/extend — add Playwright CT computed-style EXACT-equality fidelity assertions on enumerated .request-bar props (heights, radius, focus ring, method-select treatment, Send weight/shadow, labelled actions) + a thresholded screenshot diff; reuse the existing tokens.css + data-mstyle=soft fixture scope and Radix two-step dismiss gate; existing behaviour/layout CT + unit tests must stay green (accessible names Send/Save/Share/GET preserved).

acceptance_criteria.statement: The renderer shall provide the RequestBar organism module and its sibling stylesheet under the organisms directory.
The RequestBar source shall contain no design-export cruft markers such as data-om attributes, __OmT wrappers, or a tweaks-panel.
The renderer shall keep Shell as the sole writer of the document data-mstyle attribute, with the RequestBar never writing it.
WHILE the active tab changes, the RequestBar shall render the newly active tab's method and url without leaking the previous tab's values.

WHILE the URL input is rendered it shall bind its border-radius to the var(--radius) token, and WHILE it is focused it shall present the reference accent focus treatment of an accent border plus an accent ring shadow.

WHILE the method pill is rendered, the RequestBar shall present the reference bordered method-select treatment of a border, an elevated background, the var(--radius) token, and bold coloured method text via a local request-bar method override, keeping per-method colour on the global method classes.

WHEN the user operates any RequestBar control or keyboard shortcut, the RequestBar shall preserve the feature-009 behaviour unchanged including the updateActiveSpec write path, the onSend intent, markClean on Save, and the canSend empty-after-trim guard.

WHILE the RequestBar is rendered, the RequestBar shall apply the reference bar padding and gap and an approximately 32px control height from the design reference, asserted by computed-style equality in Playwright component tests.
WHILE Send is enabled the RequestBar shall render Send as a solid-primary fill at the reference font weight with the reference inset-highlight shadow and a visible aria-hidden Cmd-Enter keycap.
WHILE Send is disabled the RequestBar shall not render the Cmd-Enter keycap.

WHILE Save and Share are rendered the RequestBar shall present them as laid-out bordered actions with visible text labels beside their icons and shall keep Share a disabled no-op stub in its final slot.
The RequestBar fidelity styling shall be scoped under the request-bar selector so no other component is restyled.

WHEN the fidelity suite runs the enumerated computed-style properties shall be asserted by exact equality in a real browser via Playwright component tests backed by a thresholded screenshot diff within an explicit anti-aliasing pixel-threshold.
The touched RequestBar source shall document the request-bar-scoped fidelity treatment, the local method-select override, and the aria-hidden Cmd-Enter keycap rationale in comments.
The renderer source shall pass strict type-checking.
The codebase shall pass ESLint without new errors.
The touched RequestBar source shall contain no inline style attributes outside comments.
The RequestBar unit and component test suites shall pass.

ac_subsection_na.ci_pipeline: No CI pipeline changes; tests run via existing npm scripts (vitest/playwright), not a new pipeline stage.
ac_subsection_na.hooks_gates: No new git hooks or commit gates; the project's existing forge gates apply unchanged.

out_of_scope.content: Restoring the un-gated tokens.css .method.GET..OPTIONS{color} default rules (Approach 2 / hypothesis B from the research) is NOT done in 010 — it is a design-system-wide change deferred to a separate follow-up; the in-app pill is already coloured via Shell's default data-mstyle='soft', so 010 needs no tokens.css change.

Any behaviour change whatsoever — HTTP execution, the response view, RequestSpec read/write semantics, the onSend intent, the Cmd-Enter/Cmd-S shortcuts, dirty/markClean, the canSend guard, per-tab isolation — is excluded; 010 is presentational/CSS only.

Chrome outside the 009 RequestBar scope — Params/Auth/Headers/Body/Tests/Code sub-tabs, {{variable}} highlighting, the environment selector, the command palette, and the app header bar — is not touched.

Reproducing design/reference.html markup or its generated export cruft (data-om-* attributes, __OmT wrappers, inline styles, tweaks-panel) — fidelity is rebuilt from semantic classes bound to tokens.css, never by copying the reference DOM.
Separate dark-theme fidelity work — dark rendering is driven by the existing light/dark token pairs in tokens.css; no theme-specific geometry or layout work is in scope.
Repo-wide reformatting or unrelated housekeeping on this feature branch — it pollutes the /verify hygiene scope vs the breakdown baseline (lesson carried from features 001/002/005).

constraints.content: Only Shell.tsx writes documentElement data-mstyle (AC-6 / §4 Never)
Never use inline styles — class-based styling via cx() (§4 Never)
Prefer design tokens over literal style values (§4 Prefer)

Style exclusively via tokens-bound semantic class names from tokens.css; no inline styles; bind radius/colour/shadow/font to existing custom-properties (--radius, --accent, --border, --bg-elev, --fs-*); raw px only where no token exists (spacing). Never reproduce design-export markup (data-om-*, __OmT, tweaks-panel).

The fix is localised to RequestBar.css plus presentational RequestBar.tsx className/label edits; every reference value maps to an existing token, so no tokens.css/tokens.json change is required. Reuse the canonical .method/.{METHOD} convention (methodChipClassName, src/renderer/src/components/molecules/Tabs.tsx:117); introduce no new method-colour mechanism and no new runtime dependency.

Scope all new fidelity CSS under the .request-bar selector (mirror the 005 .tabbar-scoping precedent) so the method pill and other .method consumers (TabBar) are not regressed; the method-pill treatment is a local override, never a data-mstyle write.

Verify fidelity via tiered checks per the 005 precedent — computed-style EXACT equality on the enumerated props in Playwright CT (a real browser; jsdom cannot resolve these) plus a thresholded screenshot diff with an explicit anti-aliasing pixel-threshold (no eyeballing). CT fixtures must import tokens.css, set data-mstyle on the host, and apply the Radix two-step dismiss gate.

Where a visible text label supplies a control's accessible name (Save/Share), drop the now-redundant aria-label to avoid double-labelling; the ⌘↵ keycap is decorative and aria-hidden so the Send button's accessible name stays 'Send'. Existing CT/unit accessible-name queries (Send/Save/Share/GET) must still resolve.

All feature-009 behaviour must not regress — RequestSpec read/write, updateActiveSpec, the onSend intent, the Cmd-Enter/Cmd-S shortcut paths, dirty/markClean, the canSend empty-after-trim guard, per-tab render isolation, and Shell as the sole writer of data-mstyle (009 AC-6). The empty-state greyed-disabled Send is correct and preserved, not restyled away.

open_questions.content: Exact reference geometry values (control height ~32px, --radius 7px binding, bar padding/gap, focus-ring spread, Send weight/shadow) are pulled from design/styles.css and runtime-confirmed via design-auditor at /plan before the fidelity ACs lock to concrete numbers.

The exact screenshot-diff anti-aliasing pixel-threshold is deferred to /plan; the spec mandates an explicit threshold MUST be set (verification = computed-style equality + thresholded diff, not eyeballing), /plan fixes the concrete number (e.g. Playwright toHaveScreenshot threshold + maxDiffPixelRatio), mirroring 005 Q-2.

The deferred design-system follow-up — restoring the un-gated tokens.css .method.GET..OPTIONS{color} defaults so the pill colours in no-data-mstyle isolation (CT/stories) — is tracked separately (scope decision: localised-now, file-tokens-follow-up); it is NOT part of 010 and may be filed as its own /research or bug.

risks.risk: CT fidelity flakiness: the Radix method-dropdown dismiss arm-race plus a missing data-mstyle/tokens.css context produce intermittent false pass/fail; and jsdom cannot resolve computed/pseudo styles, so a stale CT build cache can mask a clean source as broken.
risks.mitigation: Author fidelity asserts in Playwright CT (real browser); reuse the established fixture scope (import tokens.css + set data-mstyle='soft' on host) and the two-step dismiss gate; when a build/test error names a clean file, clear playwright/.cache, node_modules/.vite, dist and re-run before editing source (cache-clearing lesson logged 2026-06-24).

risks.risk: Locking RequestBar.css to assumed reference values without a runtime check could enshrine a wrong geometry/treatment value — the diagnosis is code-confirmed against design/styles.css but not yet runtime-verified against the rendered reference.
risks.mitigation: Confirm the exact reference geometry/treatment via a runtime design-auditor screenshot + computed-style diff against design/reference.html (filled state) during /plan or /implement before locking the values; fidelity ACs assert against the confirmed values.

risks.risk: Dropping a control's aria-label while adding a visible text label could break existing CT/unit getByRole name queries (Send/Save/Share/GET) if the visible text does not supply an equivalent accessible name.
risks.mitigation: Ensure the visible label text equals the prior accessible name before removing aria-label; keep the ⌘↵ keycap aria-hidden; run the existing RequestBar CT + unit suites as a regression gate.

risks.risk: The local .request-bar__method override must out-specify the tokens.css [data-mstyle='soft'] .method.GET colour rules; a specificity miss could drop the per-method colour or the method-select treatment.
risks.mitigation: Follow the 009 .request-bar__method.method (0,2,0) specificity precedent; colour stays from the .method.{METHOD} path; assert the computed method-text colour + border treatment in CT.

approval_summary:
I've created the specification at `specs/010-request-bar-fidelity/spec.md`. Key points:
- **What changes**: Close the visual-fidelity gap between the RequestBar organism (feature 009) and design/reference.html's request bar, in BOTH the empty and filled states, with ZERO behaviour change. 009 shipped functionally correct but visually drifted: ...
- **Files affected**: 5 files across 3 areas
- **Acceptance criteria**: 19 testable criteria across 5 AC categories
- **Out of scope**: Restoring the un-gated tokens.css .method.GET..OPTIONS{color} default rules (App; Any behaviour change whatsoever — HTTP execution, the response view, RequestSpec; Chrome outside the 009 RequestBar scope — Params/Auth/Headers/Body/Tests/Code su; …

Please review and either approve or request changes. Once approved, run `/plan` to create the technical implementation plan.
plan_handoff_block:
## Manual next step — run /plan

A structured handoff (specs/010-request-bar-fidelity/handoff.json) is written for /plan. /plan auto-discovers it on its first run and reads the upstream plan-seeds — but you still launch /plan manually (there is no auto-dispatch from /specify). Restart Claude Code (exit and relaunch the CLI/app so the newly installed command is picked up), then run the command below in this repo. The spec path is explicit so /plan does not need most-recent-spec discovery:

~~~
/plan specs/010-request-bar-fidelity/spec.md
~~~

Minimum handoff data:
- Spec status: Draft
- Spec type: refactor
- AC count: 19 across 5 subsections (5.1: 2, 5.2: 3, 5.3: 8, 5.4: 0, 5.5: 0, 5.6: 1, 5.7: 5)
- Decision-point coverage: 3 answered, 0 default-applied, 0 deferred-OOS, 0 deferred-open-question
- Affected areas: 3 across src
- Out-of-scope items: 6
- Open questions: 3
- Constraints: 10
- Risks: 4
- Phase 1.5 finding coverage: 100% (all findings landed)

Reference: specs/010-request-bar-fidelity/spec.md

open_question_resolutions.resolution_text: Concrete reference values extracted from design/styles.css and fixed in the plan: .request-bar padding 12px 16px / gap 8px; control height 32px; --radius (7px) on URL input, method-select box, Send and ghost actions; URL focus = border-color var(--accent) + box-shadow 0 0 0 3px var(--accent-soft); Send var(--accent) fill weight 600 + box-shadow 0 1px 0 rgba(0,0,0,.06), inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,.15); .kbd font 11px / --text-faint / bg --bg-elev / border 1px --border / radius 3px / padding 1px 5px; method-select min-width 88px, padding 7px 10px 7px 12px, font-mono 700 11.5px. Runtime design-auditor diff confirms before lock.

Plan fixes the Playwright screenshot-diff threshold for the RequestBar fidelity snapshot: toHaveScreenshot with maxDiffPixelRatio 0.01 and per-pixel threshold 0.2 (anti-aliasing tolerance), mirroring the 005 thresholded-diff precedent; computed-style EXACT-equality assertions remain the primary gate, the screenshot diff is the backstop.
